result.stdout:
C# REPL 0.6.8+ee6780406e06ea568057b31dd5135e4ab8154099
Usage: csharprepl [OPTIONS] [@response-file.rsp] [script-file.csx] [-- <additional-arguments>]

Starts a REPL (read eval print loop) according to the provided [OPTIONS].
These [OPTIONS] can be provided at the command line, or via a [@response-file.rsp].
A [script-file.csx], if provided, will be executed before the prompt starts.

OPTIONS:
  -r <dll> or --reference <dll>:              Reference assemblies, nuget packages, and csproj files. Can be specified multiple times.
  -u <namespace> or --using <namespace>:      Add using statement. Can be specified multiple times.
  -f <framework> or --framework <framework>:  Reference a shared framework.
                                              Available shared frameworks: 
                                               - Microsoft.NETCore.App (default)
                                               - Microsoft.AspNetCore.App
  -t <theme.json> or --theme <theme.json>:    Read a theme file for syntax highlighting. Respects the NO_COLOR standard.
                                              Available default themes: 
                                               - themes/VisualStudio_Dark.json (default)
                                               - themes/VisualStudio_BlueExtraContrast.json
                                               - themes/VisualStudio_Light.json
                                               - themes/dracula.json
  --useTerminalPaletteTheme:                  Uses terminal palette colors for syntax highlighting. Respects the NO_COLOR standard.
  --prompt:                                   Formatted prompt string.
  --useUnicode:                               Use UTF8 output encoding and unicode character decorations (requires terminal support).
  --usePrereleaseNugets:                      Allows prerelease NuGet versions when searching for the latest package version.
  --streamPipedInput:                         If input is piped via stdin, evaluate it line by line instead of in one batch.
  --tabSize <width>:                          Width of tab character.
  --culture <culture name>:                   Culture to use for access to the MSDN documentation. Defaults to the current culture.

  Key Bindings
  --triggerCompletionListKeys <key-binding>:  Key binding to trigger the completion list. Can be specified multiple times.
  --newLineKeys <key-binding>:                Key binding to insert a newline character. Can be specified multiple times.
  --submitPromptKeys <key-binding>:           Key binding to submit the prompt. Can be specified multiple times.
  --submitPromptDetailedKeys <key-binding>:   Key binding to submit the prompt with detailed output. Can be specified multiple times.

  Open AI
  --openAIApiKey:                             OpenAI API key. Alternatively, set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.
  --openAIPrompt:                             OpenAI prompt to prefix to all code submissions
  --openAIModel:                              OpenAI model configuration
  --openAIHistoryCount:                       Maximum number of previous REPL entries to send to OpenAI as context.

  Help and Diagnostics
  --trace:                                    Produce a trace file in the current directory, for CSharpRepl bug reports.
  -v or --version:                            Show version number and exit.
  -h or --help:                               Show this help and exit.

@response-file.rsp:
  A file, with extension .rsp, containing the above command line [OPTIONS], one option per line.
  Command line options will also be loaded from /tmp/inspectra-static-csharprepl-0.6.8-33f70a3a2086464484e05625b502319f/xdg-config/.csharprepl/config.rsp
  Run 'csharprepl --configure' to launch this file in your editor.

script-file.csx:
  A file, with extension .csx, containing lines of C# to evaluate before starting the REPL.
  Arguments to this script can be passed as <additional-arguments> and will be available in a global `args` variable.
